title: Points, lines, and planes
description: Practice the relationship between points, lines, and planes. For example, given the drawing of a plane and points within 3D space, determine whether the points are colinear or coplanar.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is another way to name line $\ell$?**



![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/542f4c15b73efed65411c8e31bba4d341757175b)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Lines are named using any two points on the line. The order doesn't matter.
The points must have the $\leftrightarrow$ above because we're naming a line, not a ray or a segment.
Another way to name line $\ell$ is $\overleftrightarrow{ML}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Are the points $O, M,$ and $U$ collinear?**



![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/351587eca75eb9d0be086635017b8da20c97d98e)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Collinear means that they lie on the same line.
Can you draw a straight line through points $O, M,$ and $U$?
No, points $O, M,$ and $U$ are not collinear.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Are the points $H, L,$ and $U$ collinear?**



![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b952366b29eee0347c8111083d0dcc82f29a3c0f)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Collinear means that they lie on the same line.
Can you draw a straight line through points $H, L,$ and $U$?
Yes, points $H, L,$ and $U$ are collinear.

assessment_items.question_content:

assessment_items.question_content:
**Are the points $M$ and $O$ collinear?**



![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6bc58059b94a06c35173b8ea5c6ee2f00e15f935)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Through any two points, there is exactly one line.
Points can be collinear even if the line isn't drawn in the figure.
Can you draw a straight line through points $M$ and $O$? Actually, can you draw a straight line through any two points?
Yes, points $M$ and $O$ are collinear.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Are the points $O, X,$ and $U$ coplanar?**



![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/390918058e6b5b459507a5b174f02619f7c59e53)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Coplanar points are points that all lie on the same plane.
Can a flat surface pass through all the points without bending?
Yes, there is always at least one flat surface that passes through any three points.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Are the points $H, N,$ and $U$ coplanar?**



![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/25974aab2dd7ad28852bcb63c4584b523b0b9425)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Coplanar points are points that all lie on the same plane.
Can a flat surface pass through all the points without bending?
Yes, there is always at least one flat surface that passes through any three points.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Are the points $A, M,$ and $I$ coplanar?**



![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/cafe78d44ab24f5286baf74ac8d97edd4c306935)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Coplanar points are points that all lie on the same plane.
Can a flat surface pass through all the points without bending?
Yes, there is always at least one flat surface that passes through any three points.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Are the points $G, C, A,$ and $Y$ coplanar?**



![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/abfed6e0e1a4947343f034d57b6f9db32a64b60e)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Coplanar points are points that all lie on the same plane.
Can a flat surface pass through all the points without bending?
Yes, points $G, C, A,$ and $Y$ all lie within a single flat surface. In this case, plane $\mathcal{R}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Are the points $E, I,$ and $A$ coplanar?**



![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c0a95b1b44bb2e3ae6f1c8be110229c5ae44848f)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Coplanar points are points that all lie on the same plane.
Can a flat surface pass through all the points without bending?
Yes, there is always at least one flat surface that passes through any three points.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Are the points $M, B, I,$ and $O$ coplanar?**



![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c9fe055d223ce6855e732f217bdf8a38b739dd86)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Coplanar points are points that all lie on the same plane.
Can a flat surface pass through all the points without bending?
No, any flat surface that includes three of the points won't include the fourth. For example, points $M, B,$ and $O$ are in plane $\mathcal{R}$, but point $I$ is not.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Are the points $U, H,$ and $I$ coplanar?**



![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d83828a4ddb03204dec4289b2cd761dccf4e3eab)

Can a flat surface pass through all the points without bending?

assessment_items.question_content:
**Are the points $W, V, N,$ and $O$ coplanar?**



![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/344a5a5a6011c680d2aa10d4a071e64f8f43e901)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Coplanar points are points that all lie on the same plane.
Can a flat surface pass through all the points without bending?
No, any flat surface that includes three of the points won't include the fourth. For example, points $W, V,$ and $O$ are in plane $\mathcal{P}$, but point $N$ is not.
